---
title: CV - [First Name Last Name]
author: "First Last"
format:
  quarto-cv-typst: default
date: last-modified # use last-modified for auto update, or set a specific date (e.g. 2026-01-01)
date-prefix: "Last updated: " # leave empty ("") to hide the prefix
execute:
  echo: false

# =============================================================================
# STEP 1 — Choose a theme (uncomment one line)
# =============================================================================
# Each theme sets colors, fonts, spacing and layout automatically.
# Only ONE line should be active at a time. Comment out the others.
metadata-files:
  - _extensions/quarto-cv/themes/classic-blue.yml      # Blue professional — light sidebar, blue headings (default)
  # - _extensions/quarto-cv/themes/forest-green.yml    # Natural green — mint sidebar, elegant green accents
  # - _extensions/quarto-cv/themes/minimal-dark.yml    # Dark sidebar — high contrast, modern Inter font
  # - _extensions/quarto-cv/themes/warm-terracotta.yml # Earthy terracotta — sand sidebar, warm brown tones

# =============================================================================
# STEP 2 — Your personal info
# =============================================================================
# All fields are optional — only include what you want to show.
# Unused fields are automatically hidden from the sidebar.
sidebar:
  photo: "assets/img/image.png"                    # Profile photo (appears at top of sidebar)
  phone: "XX XX XX XX XX"                        # Phone number (appears in Contact section)
  email: "fname.lname@email.com"                 # Email address (appears in Contact section)
  birthdate: "DD/MM/YYYY"                        # Date of birth (appears in Contact section)
  city: "City, Country"                          # Location (appears in Contact section)
  nationality: "Nationality"                     # Nationality (appears in Contact section)
  website: "https://www.mywebsite.com"           # Personal/portfolio website URL
  # website-display: "My Website"                # Optional: custom label (default: domain name)
  permit: "Driving License B"                    # Driving license type (appears in Contact section)
  linkedin: "https://linkedin.com/in/username"   # LinkedIn profile URL (appears in Contact section)
  linkedin-display: "\\@username"                # Optional: custom label (default: @username)
  github: "https://github.com/username"          # GitHub profile URL (appears in Contact section)
  github-display: "\\@username"                  # Optional: custom label (default: @username)

# =============================================================================
# STEP 3 — Sidebar sections (reorder or comment out to hide)
# =============================================================================
# - Change the order of sections by moving blocks up or down.
# - Comment out an entire block (- id: ...) to hide that section.
# - Each section accepts a style: block to override spacing, font, colors.
#   See the ADVANCED section below or the README for all style options.
#
# You can also add your own custom sections with any id and title:
#
#   - id: my-section
#     title: "My Custom Section"
#     items:
#       - "Free text item"
#       - name: "Key"
#         value: "Value"
#       - name: "Category"
#         subitems:
#           - "Sub-item 1"
#           - "Sub-item 2"
#
sidebar-sections:

  # --- Profile photo --- (special section: displays photo from sidebar.photo)
  - id: photo
    style:
      section-before: 2em        # space above photo
      photo-size: 100pt          # diameter of photo (circle or square)
      photo-radius: 50%          # 50% = circle, 0% = square, other % = rounded corners
      photo-border: true         # show/hide border around photo
      photo-border-width: 4pt    # thickness of photo border
      section-after: 4em         # space below photo

  # --- Contact info --- (pulls fields from sidebar: data above)
  # Available types: phone, email, birthdate, city, nationality, permit, website, linkedin, github
  # Change order, add/remove as needed. Only fields defined in sidebar: will appear.
  - id: contact
    title: "Contact"
    items:
      - type: phone              # Shows sidebar.phone (with icon)
      - type: email              # Shows sidebar.email (with icon)
      - type: birthdate          # Shows sidebar.birthdate (with icon)
      - type: city               # Shows sidebar.city (with icon)
      - type: nationality        # Shows sidebar.nationality (with icon)
      - type: permit             # Shows sidebar.permit (with icon)
      - type: linkedin           # Shows sidebar.linkedin URL (with icon + custom label)
    style:
      item-after: 0em            # no space between contact items
      section-after: 3em         # space below entire Contact section
      # icon-color: "#ff9500"    # OPTIONAL: override icon color for this section
      # icon-size: 10pt          # OPTIONAL: override icon size
      # title-size: 18pt         # OPTIONAL: override section title size
      #text-size: 9pt           # OPTIONAL: override text size for this section

  # --- Free text + link --- (custom section for additional info)
  # Any combination of free text and contact links
  - id: details
    title: "More Info"
    items:
      - "[Visit my website for more details about my background and work.]"
      - type: website            # Shows sidebar.website link with icon
    style:
      text-size: 9.5pt

  # --- Language / level pairs --- (name ↔ value format)
  # Perfect for: Languages + proficiency, Certifications + year, etc.
  - id: languages
    title: "Languages"
    items:
      - name: "[Language 1]"     # left column
        value: "[Level]"         # right column (e.g. "Fluent", "Native")
      - name: "[Language 2]"
        value: "[Level]"
    style:
      text-size: 10.5pt

  # --- Plain bullet list --- (simple text items)
  # Great for: key competencies, expertise areas, strengths
  - id: strengths
    title: "Strengths"
    items:
      - "[Strength 1]"           # bullet point
      - "[Strength 2]"           # bullet point
      - "[Strength 3]"           # bullet point
      - "[Strength 4]"           # bullet point
    style:
      text-size: 10.5pt

  # --- Nested interests --- (category + subcategories)
  # Perfect for: hobbies, interests, research areas with sub-topics
  - id: interests
    title: "Interests"
    items:
      - name: "[Interest 1]"     # category heading
        subitems:
          - "[Sub-interest 1]"   # sub-item 1
          - "[Sub-interest 2]"   # sub-item 2
          - "[Sub-interest 3]"   # sub-item 3
      - name: "[Interest 2]"     # another category
        subitems:
          - "[Sub-interest 1]"
          - "[Sub-interest 2]"
      - name: "[Interest 3]"     # category with no subitems is OK
    style:
      text-size: 10.5pt

  # --- Skill categories --- (category label + comma-separated inline list)
  # Great for: programming languages, tools, frameworks grouped by type
  - id: skills
    title: "IT Skills"
    items:
      - category: "Languages"    # category label (e.g. "Programming Languages")
        items: "[Language 1, Language 2]"  # comma-separated skills on one line
      - category: "Tools"
        items: "[Tool 1, Tool 2, Tool 3]"
    style:
      text-size: 10.5pt
      # section-before: 5em      # OPTIONAL: add space above this section

  # --- Example: commented-out optional sections ---

  # - id: certifications
  #   title: "Certifications"
  #   items:
  #     - name: "AWS Solutions Architect"
  #       value: "2023"
  #     - name: "Google Cloud Professional"
  #       value: "2024"
  #   style:
  #     text-size: 8pt

  # - id: networks
  #   title: "Networks"
  #   items:
  #     - type: linkedin
  #     - type: github

# =============================================================================
# STEP 4 — Header: name, job title and objective (shown at top of the CV)
# =============================================================================
# These fields appear as a prominent header at the start of the document.
# Colors and sizes inherit from the active theme (see STEP 1).
# All fields below are optional — omit to hide.
cv-header:
  name: "[First Name Last Name]"                  # Your full name (bold, large)
  title: "[Job Title or Research Position]"       # Current/target position (bold, accent color)
  objective: >-                                   # Professional summary (2-3 sentences)
    [Your professional goal. Describe your background, motivations and
    what you are looking for in 2-3 sentences.]   # Shown in a styled box with border

  # === OPTIONAL COLOR/SIZE OVERRIDES ===
  # Uncomment to override theme defaults for this header only.
  # If not specified, theme colors apply.
  # name-color: "#000000"                         # Name text color
  # name-size: "14pt"                             # Name font size
  # title-color: "#2563eb"                        # Title color (uses cv-theme.main.title-color by default)
  # title-size: "18pt"                            # Title font size
  # objective-bg: "#f1f5f9"                       # Objective box background (uses cv-layout.sidebar-bg by default)
  # objective-stroke-color: "#2563eb"             # Objective box border color (uses cv-theme.main.title-color by default)
  # objective-stroke-width: "2pt"                 # Objective box border thickness

# =============================================================================
# ADVANCED — Override theme values (uncomment to customize)
# =============================================================================
# These blocks let you fine-tune the active theme without editing the theme
# file. Only the keys you define here are overridden — the rest come from
# the theme preset chosen in STEP 1.

# --- Theme: Colors & fonts ---
# cv-theme:
#   main-font: "Poppins"              # main content font (Inter also bundled)
#   title-font: "Poppins"             # sidebar section title font
#   text-font: "Poppins"              # sidebar body text font
#
#   header:                           # CV header styling
#     name-color: "#0f172a"
#     title-color: "#2563eb"
#     objective-bg: "#eff6ff"
#     objective-stroke-color: "#2563eb"
#     objective-stroke-width: "2pt"
#
#   sidebar:                          # colors for the LEFT column
#     title-color: "#2563eb"          # section titles (Contact, Languages…) + photo border
#     text-color: "#1e293b"           # plain items, names, key labels
#     accent-color: "#64748b"         # values (language levels), sub-items
#     link-color: "#2563eb"           # phone, email, @username, website links
#
#   main:                             # colors for the RIGHT column
#     title-color: "#2563eb"          # H1 headings (Education, Experience…)
#     subtitle-color: "#1e293b"       # H2 headings (Position, Degree…)
#     text-color: "#1e293b"           # body text
#     # link-color: none              # leave unset → use inline color style
#
#   headings:                         # font sizes in the right column
#     h1: 14pt                        # section titles
#     h2: 11pt                        # entry titles / positions
#     h3: 10pt                        # details (company, institution…)
#     normal: 10pt                    # body text

# --- Sidebar spacing (global defaults for all sections) ---
# sidebar-defaults:
#   text-size: 9pt                    # body text size
#   title-size: 11pt                  # section title size
#   icon-size: 11pt                   # icon size
#   title-after: 1em                  # space below section title
#   item-after: 1em                   # space between items
#   section-after: 3em                # space below entire section

# --- Layout & page setup ---
# cv-layout:
#   sidebar-width: 30%                # left column width (%, cm, or fr)
#   main-width: 2.5fr                 # right column width
#   gutter: 1cm                       # space between columns
#   sidebar-bg: "#f1f5f9"             # sidebar background
#   main-bg: "#ffffff"                # main area background
#   margins:
#     top: 0.5cm
#     bottom: 0.5cm
#     left: 0.5cm
#     right: 0.5cm
#   timeline:
#     dot-size: "3pt"                 # dot size in timeline sections
#     line-width: "1pt"               # vertical line width
#     # dot-color: "#2563eb"          # optional dot color override
#     # line-color: "#94a3b8"         # optional line color override

# --- Per-section style overrides (uncomment to customize individual sections) ---
# Example: override the "Contact" section styling
# sidebar-sections:
#   - id: contact
#     title: "Contact"
#     items:
#       - type: phone
#       - type: email
#     style:
#       text-size: 9pt
#       title-size: 11pt
#       icon-size: 10pt
#       icon-color: "#ff9500"         # custom icon color for this section
#       item-after: 0.5em             # tighter spacing
#       section-after: 2em

# --- ADVANCED CUSTOMIZATION EXAMPLES (see examples/advanced-example.qmd for full showcase) ---
#
# You can customize virtually everything. Here are examples of what's possible:
#
# 1. PHOTO SHAPE — Circle (default) or square
#    - id: photo
#      style:
#        photo-radius: 50%             # 50% = circle (default)
#        photo-radius: 0%              # 0% = square
#        photo-size: 80pt              # adjust diameter
#
# 2. PER-SECTION CUSTOM COLORS
#    - id: skills
#      style:
#        icon-color: "#3b82f6"         # override icons color
#        text-color: "#1e293b"         # override text color
#        category-color: "#8b5cf6"     # for skill categories
#
# 3. PER-SECTION CUSTOM FONTS
#    - id: programming
#      style:
#        text-font: "Courier New"      # monospace for code skills
#        title-font: "Courier New"
#        text-size: 8pt
#
# 4. WIDE SIDEBAR & CUSTOM BACKGROUND
#    cv-layout:
#      sidebar-width: 40%              # wider sidebar (vs default 30%)
#      sidebar-bg: "#0f172a"           # dark sidebar background
#      main-bg: "#f9fafb"              # light main content
#      gutter: 2cm                     # wider space between columns
#
# 5. DIFFERENT FONTS GLOBALLY
#    cv-theme:
#      main-font: "Inter"              # main content font
#      title-font: "Poppins"           # section titles
#      text-font: "Poppins"            # body text
#
# 6. CUSTOM TIMELINE STYLING
#    cv-layout:
#      timeline:
#        dot-size: "4pt"               # bigger dots
#        line-width: "2pt"             # thicker line
#        dot-color: "#ef4444"          # red dots
#        line-color: "#fca5a5"         # light red line
#
# See examples/advanced-example.qmd for a complete implementation of these features.
---

# Education

::: {.timeline }

## YYYY - YYYY | [Degree Title]

*[Institution, City] | [Grade/Honours]*

[Description of the program content.]

## YYYY - YYYY | [Degree Title]

*[Institution, City] | [Grade/Honours]*

[Description of the program content.]

## YYYY - YYYY | [Degree Title]

*[Institution, City] | [Grade/Honours]*

:::

# Professional Experience

::: {.timeline levels="2,3,4"}

## Mon. YYYY - Mon. YYYY | [Job Title]

*[Lab / Organization, City]*

[Description of the mission and tasks performed ([Project Name]).]

## Mon. YYYY - Mon. YYYY | [Job Title]

*[Lab / Organization, City]*

[Description of the mission and tasks performed ([Project Name]).]

## Mon. YYYY - Mon. YYYY | [Job Title]

*[Lab / Organization, City]*

[Description of the mission and tasks performed ([Project Name]).]

## Mon. YYYY - Mon. YYYY | [Job Title]

*[Lab / Organization, City]*

[Description of the mission and tasks performed ([Project Name]).]

:::
